name: Zal
description: Zal, also known as Zal-e Zaranj, is a legendary hero in Persian mythology, prominently featured in Ferdowsi's Shahnameh (Book of Kings). He is the son of Sam, a renowned Iranian paladin, but his birth was marked by a significant difference: Zal was born with white hair. Sam, ashamed and believing him to be a demon child, abandoned Zal on Mount Alborz. Simurgh, a mythical bird of immense wisdom and compassion, discovered the infant and raised him as her own. Years later, Sam, prompted by dreams and remorse, sought out his son. Witnessing Zal's strength and noble character, Sam embraced him, bringing him back to the kingdom. Zal grew to be a mighty warrior, known for his wisdom, courage, and strategic prowess. He married Rudabeh, a princess of Kabulistan, and their union produced Rostam, arguably the greatest hero in Persian legend. Zal played a crucial role in the upbringing and guidance of Rostam, ensuring that he was trained in the arts of war and instilled with a strong sense of justice. Zal's influence extended beyond his immediate family, as he served as a trusted advisor to several kings and played a pivotal part in maintaining peace and order within the Persian empire. His legacy is one of overcoming adversity, embodying wisdom, and fostering the next generation of heroes.
subtitle: The White-Haired Hero of Persia

enemies.name: Those who threaten the Persian Empire

keyMyths.title: Abandonment and Rescue by the Simurgh
keyMyths.description: Zal's birth with white hair led his father to abandon him on Mount Alborz. The Simurgh, a mythical bird, found him and raised him, showcasing her compassion and wisdom.

keyMyths.title: Reunion with Sam
keyMyths.description: Sam, guided by dreams, sought out Zal and, impressed by his strength and nobility, brought him back to the kingdom. This marked Zal's acceptance and integration into Persian society.

keyMyths.title: Marriage to Rudabeh and Birth of Rostam
keyMyths.description: Zal's marriage to Rudabeh, a princess from Kabulistan, defied societal norms and resulted in the birth of Rostam, the greatest hero of Persian mythology, solidifying Zal's legacy.

sources.author: Ferdowsi
sources.description: The primary source for the story of Zal and other Persian mythological figures.
sources.text: The primary source for the story of Zal and other Persian mythological figures.
